title: Technology and cultural transfers during the Crusades
description: Check your knowledge of technology and cultural transfers during the Crusades!
topic_path: Technology and cultural transfers during the Crusades

assessment_items.question_content:
The **crusades** were a series of European military expeditions 

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints: Jerusalem was an important city to Christians, but crusades had other targets, too.

choices.content: that sought to recapture the Holy Land, assist Christians living under Muslim rule, and convert or conquer pagans and heretics
focused specifically on the capture of Jerusalem for Christianity
aimed at expanding European economic power

assessment_items.question_content:
Relationships between Christians and Muslims during the crusades were

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints: Some crusades targeted Jews, pagans, and heretics, but this did not improve relations between Christians and Muslims.

choices.content: entirely hostile; all interactions were violent
frequently changing as dictated by local circumstances
friendly; most violence was directed at other groups

assessment_items.question_content:
The crusades altered trade patterns in the Mediterranean Sea by

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints: Prior to the Crusades, much of the Mediterranean Sea was under Muslim control.

choices.content: establishing European Christian outposts in previously Muslim and Byzantine regions
making all trade in Europe go through Venice and Genoa
only allowing Latin Christians to trade

hints: Much of Europe’s wealth prior to the crusades was in land. Coins were in short supply.

choices.content: easily accomplished; many people willingly donated money to the expeditions
easily accomplished; crusades were economically profitable
difficult; leaders often had to sell their assets, or secure large loans

assessment_items.question_content:
The crusades changed Europe by

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints: Increased economic interaction tends to lead to diffusion of knowledge between societies.

choices.content: expanding its economic networks, thus increasing its access to knowledge and technology
closing it economically, leading to decreased development
increasing interactions with people of other religious backgrounds, leading to more tolerance for diverse religious beliefs
